agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by optimizing hydrophobic interactions at position 1

report.hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by optimizing hydrophobic interactions at position 11 and electrostatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 residues, while preserving strong SSTR2 binding.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis remains valid, but we must prioritize structural quality

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but we must prioritize structural quality and off-target disruption. The focus should be on generating candidates with high pLDDT and strong ddG while maintaining selectivity through strategic mutations at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding (e.g., -20.8 is marginal). Tightening the threshold will filter out weaker binders and prioritize stronger SSTR2 binders.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to introduce mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
